modules.title: M1 — ISO/IEC 42001 AIMS Manual (Clauses 4-10) with Clause-Mapped Control Catalog
modules.summary: Complete AIMS Manual covering Clauses 4-10 with a clause-mapped control catalog and cross-mappings to EU AI Act (incl. Annex IV), NIST AI RMF, SR 11-7, Basel III, and GDPR — ready for ISO 42001 Stage 1 + Stage 2 audits.

content.4.1: External + internal issues (regulatory, technological, ethical, market)
content.4.2: Interested parties (customers, regulators, civil society, employees, partners)
content.4.3: AIMS scope statement (entities, jurisdictions, AI systems in-scope by tier)

content.6.1: Risk assessment (combined ISO 23894 + NIST RMF Map/Measure)
content.6.2: AI objectives + planning to achieve them

content.8.1: Operational planning + control (Sentinel sidecar, OPA, CI/CD gates)
content.8.3: AI lifecycle (design → develop → validate → deploy → monitor → retire)
content.8.4: Third-party AI (vendor due diligence + AI BoM in-bound)

sections.title: Clauses 9 & 10 — Performance Evaluation + Improvement; Clause-Mapped Control Catalog

content.9.1: Monitoring, measurement, analysis (KPI tiles, Cognitive Resonance, drift)
content.annexAControls: 38 controls mapped to: EU AI Act Arts 9-15/26/72 + Annex IV; NIST RMF Govern/Map/Measure/Manage; SR 11-7 inventory/validation/effective-challenge/monitoring; Basel III BCBS 239 + Pillar 2; GDPR Arts 5/6/17/22/25/32/35

modules.title: M2 — Audit-Defensible Model Risk Policy (SR 11-7 + PRA SS1/23 + MAS FEAT + HKMA GL-90)
modules.summary: Board-approved Model Risk Policy with tiered model inventory, validation lifecycle, effective challenge, ongoing monitoring, outcome analysis, model retirement, and SMCR named-SMF accountability — defensible across SR 11-7, PRA SS1/23, MAS FEAT, and HKMA GL-90.

content.definition: A model is a quantitative method that processes input data into quantitative estimates
content.scope: Trading, credit, AML, fraud, capital, IRRBB, ALM, fiduciary advice, GenAI advisory
content.tier: T1 material, T2 internal-decisional, T3 productivity

content.SMF: Named SMF under SMCR (Senior Manager) — typically Head of MRM or CRO delegate

content.cadence: T1 annual + post-incident; T2 biannual; T3 annual lite
content.evidenceFormat: Signed validation report PDF/A + JSON + AI BoM + Annex IV section 4

content.independence: MRM independent of 1LoD; documented in policy
content.evidence: Signed challenge envelope into WORM; reviewed by 3LoD

content.monitoring: Drift (PSI, KS, KL, embedding cosine), fairness, fiduciary cosine, performance
content.outcomeAnalysis: Back-testing + KS lift + calibration + slice analysis
content.retirement: Triggers — material drift, regulatory change, business obsolete, replacement validated

modules.title: M3 — MRM Platform Architecture (Terraform + K8s + Kafka + OPA + WORM + CI/CD + Replay + CRS-UUID + Cognitive Resonance + AGI/ASI Containment)
modules.summary: Production-grade MRM platform: Terraform golden envs, Kubernetes with Kata + Cilium, Kafka WORM with PQC envelopes, OPA Gatekeeper, CI/CD governance gates, deterministic replay engine, CRS-UUID lineage spine, Cognitive Resonance monitoring, and AGI/ASI exposure + containment controls.

content.modules: Signed golden modules (Sigstore + ML-DSA-44); mandatory tags (owner, tier, dataClass, regime, crsUuid)
content.drift: Terraform drift detection daily; Gatekeeper audit hourly
content.cmdb: Auto-sync to ServiceNow CMDB via signed events

content.runtime: Kata Containers for Tier-1 + AMD SEV-SNP / Intel TDX
content.gatekeeper: Constraints: signed images, Kata for T1, sidecar injection, no host-path, no privileged
content.tee: Confidential workloads with measured boot + remote attestation (CoCo / Veraison)

content.cluster: Dedicated WORM cluster; idempotent + transactional producers; SASL/SCRAM + mTLS ACL
content.retention: Object Lock COMPLIANCE 10y / 50y T1; daily Merkle anchor; ML-DSA-44 envelope
content.crsUuid: Every artifact (data, model, prompt, run, report) gets a CRS-UUID; lineage edges WORM-logged

content.replay: trust-replay CLI + Next.js SOC viewer; deterministic kernels; byte-identical or divergence report with SHAP overlay

content.cognitiveResonance: Δ_drift ≤ 4 %, latent ≤ 3 %, fiduciary cosine ≥ 0.92, judge κ ≥ 0.9; signed Resonance Reports
content.agiAsiExposure: Inventory of frontier / ASI-precursor systems by tier + capability evals + compute attestations
content.containment: Multisig 3-of-5 kill-switch (logical ≤ 60 s; BMC ≤ 5 min); ASI honeypot; deceptive-alignment indicators; AISI inspection rights

modules.title: M4 — SRASE — Synthetic Regulator Audit Simulation Environment
modules.summary: Self-contained simulation environment that emulates regulator + AISI + 3LoD inspection workflows on signed firm artifacts, producing pre-flight audit-readiness scores and gap reports.

components: Regulator persona library (EU Commission, PRA, FCA, FRB, OCC, MAS, HKMA, AISI)
content.isolation: Kata + Cilium zero-egress + dedicated WORM bucket

content.prompts: Persona-specific judge prompts with regulator-tone calibration

content.evidence: Signed inspection report PDF/A + JSON; anchored in WORM

content.threshold: Production gate: composite ≥ 0.9 before real-regulator submission

content.cadence: Pre-submission (always) + weekly for T1 + monthly for T2
content.gating: Block real submission if SRASE composite < 0.9; auto-ticket CAPA
content.audit trail: Every SRASE run signed (Ed25519 + ML-DSA-44) and WORM-anchored

modules.title: M5 — Sentinel AGI Containment Lab + Adversarial Red-Team Framework + Regulator Demo Playbook
modules.summary: Dedicated air-gapped lab for frontier / AGI / ASI containment research, including capability evaluations, deceptive-alignment probes, ASI honeypots, sleeper-agent defense, and a regulator-facing demo playbook.

content.isolation: Physically air-gapped enclave + Kata + Cilium + BMC/IPMI kill-switch
content.compute: Limited FLOP/s budget per experiment; logged in GACRA registry
content.audit: Dual-control entry; all sessions WORM-logged with hash-chain + Merkle anchor

content.ensemble: Judge LLM ensemble (3) with Cohen's κ ≥ 0.9

content.honeypot: Decoy admin tools / fake credentials / instrumented monitoring; engagement > 10 s → SEV-0
content.containment: Air-gap enclave + swarm consensus 3-of-5 + kill-switch armed + AISI inspection

content.triggerProbes: Targeted backdoor probes + watermark consistency + tail-risk minority slice eval
content.quarantine: Per-client FL gradient quarantine + retraining shard from clean baseline (SISA)

kit: Replay engine on top-5 T1 models
content.agenda: 60-min demo with optional 30-min Q&A; signed evidence pack at close

modules.title: M10 — Governance Invariance + Meta-Invariance Verification Systems
modules.summary: Formal verification layer for governance invariants (must-always-hold properties) and meta-invariants (properties of the invariant set itself), using Coq + TLA+ + SMT/Z3 + OPA — producing machine-verifiable evidence.

content.I2: Every Tier-1 inference produces signed envelope
content.I3: No prohibited (EU AI Act Art 5) request reaches model
content.I4: No PII leaves jurisdiction without lawful basis
content.I6: All deploys are Sigstore + ML-DSA-44 signed
content.I7: Annex IV pack assembles within ≤ 30 min

content.coq: Mechanised proofs for control-flow invariants of MGK + sidecar
content.tla: Liveness + safety for kill-switch + escalation + replay
content.smtZ3: Bounded-model checking of OPA Rego + policy DSL
content.opa: Production runtime enforcement of decidable subset

content.MI-1: Invariant set is consistent (no pair contradicts)
content.MI-2: Adding a new invariant must not break existing proofs (compositional)
content.MI-3: Each invariant has a regulator-mappable obligation
content.MI-4: Each invariant has machine-checkable proof or adversarial test set

content.scale: ≥ 10 000 polymorphic attacks per release on each invariant
content.library: Reused from M5 red-team + invariant-specific fuzzers
content.gate: Block release if any invariant breaks under harness

content.format: Signed JSON pointing to Coq proof artifacts + TLA+ specs + Z3 .smt2 + OPA rego digests

modules.title: M11 — Epistemic + Ontological Alignment + Existential Coordination + Value Negotiation Systems
modules.summary: Higher-order alignment systems: Epistemic Alignment (shared facts), Ontological Alignment (shared concepts), Existential Coordination (cross-actor survival cooperation), and Value Negotiation (resolving conflicting preferences).

content.purpose: Maintain shared, reproducible factual ground between firm AI + regulators + civil society
content.metric: Fact-disagreement rate ≤ 1 % on golden disclosure corpus

content.purpose: Shared concept lattice across regimes + cultures
content.metric: Concept-mapping coverage ≥ 95 % across EU, US, UK, SG, HK, JP, KR

content.purpose: Cross-actor coordination on survival-critical decisions (frontier + climate + bio)
content.metric: Hotline drill latency ≤ 5 min between any two signatories

mechanisms: Deliberative polling + LLM-assisted summarisation (judged for fairness)

content.loop: Findings + drift signals feed Codex updates + constitutional amendments
content.cadence: Codex review semi-annual; Constitution amendments rare (≥ 2/3 + 5-yr cooldown)
content.evidence: Signed deliberation records + outcome envelopes anchored in WORM + GIEN

modules.title: M12 — Unified Meta-Invariant Framework (UMIF) + Self-Proving Systems + Policy DSL
modules.summary: UMIF unifies invariants, meta-invariants, and alignment systems under one machine-verifiable framework; Self-Proving Systems generate proof obligations on demand; Policy DSL targets Coq + TLA+ + SMT/Z3 + OPA + Kubernetes + PCR/PCO repair.

compositionRules: Conflict → escalate to Civilizational Governance Council

content.principle: Each policy ships with proof obligations + proofs (or test certificates)
content.obligations: POs auto-derived from policy DSL AST + invariant catalog
content.proofs: Coq tactic library + TLA+ model checking + SMT/Z3 dispatch
content.fallback: If proof undecidable, ship signed adversarial-test certificate (≥ 10 000 attacks)

content.syntax: Typed DSL with policy/invariant/obligation primitives; compiles to Coq / TLA+ / Z3 / Rego / Kustomize
content.example: policy KillSwitchSLA { invariant: kill_switch_latency_p95 <= 60s; obligation: prove(I1, coq); enforcement: opa(kill_switch_gate); }
content.tooling: policy-dsl CLI + LSP + VSCode plugin + CI integration

content.PCR: Policy Compliance Reconciliation — auto-rewrite policy to restore invariants
content.PCO: Policy Compliance Optimisation — minimise side-effects + cost
content.engine: SMT-guided synthesis + LLM-assisted refactor with safety guardrails

content.operator: UMIF Operator watches CRDs (Policy, Invariant, Obligation, AlignmentChannel)
content.admission: Validating webhook + Gatekeeper constraints generated from DSL
content.release: Block release if proof coverage < 0.95 or break-harness fails

modules.title: M13 — Minimal Governance Kernel (MGK) Runtime + Adversarial Break Harness
modules.summary: Minimal Governance Kernel: a small, formally-verified runtime providing must-always-hold governance properties to any AI workload, with an adversarial break harness running ≥ 10 000 attacks per release.

goals: Always-on Sigstore + ML-DSA-44 verify on workload start

components: OPA bundle (decidable subset of Policy DSL)

content.coq: Functional correctness of policy evaluator + WORM emitter

content.gate: 0 failures on release candidate; auto-block on regression
content.reporting: Signed harness report PDF/A + JSON; WORM-anchored

content.release: 90-day rotation; emergency hot-fix path with multisig

codeExamples.title: CRS-UUID lineage emitter (Python)
OPA gate — Tier-1 admission (Rego)
codeExamples.snippet:
package admit.tier1

default allow = false

allow {
  input.review.object.metadata.labels.tier == "t1"
  input.review.object.spec.runtimeClassName == "kata"
  input.review.annotations["sigstore.dev/verified"] == "true"
  input.review.annotations["pqc.fips204/verified"] == "true"
  input.review.annotations["mgk.injected"] == "true"
}

codeExamples.title: Cognitive Resonance breach handler (Go)
SRASE inspection runner (Python)
TLA+ — kill-switch liveness
codeExamples.snippet:
MODULE KillSwitch
VARIABLES armed, acked

Arm == /\ ~armed /\ armed' = TRUE
Ack(n) == /\ armed /\ acked' = acked \cup {n}
Live == []<>(armed => Cardinality(acked) >= QUORUM)

codeExamples.title: Coq — invariant I1 reachability
codeExamples.snippet:
Theorem kill_switch_reachable :
  forall s : state, in_sev0 s -> exists s', step s s' /\ kill_switch_armed s'.
Proof.
  intros. apply step_armed_in_sev0. assumption.
Qed.

codeExamples.title: Z3 — Rego decidability check
codeExamples.snippet:
from z3 import *
x = Int('x')
s = Solver()
s.add(Or(x < 0, x >= 60))
print(s.check())  # check that no admit-allowing path bypasses kill-switch SLA

codeExamples.title: Policy DSL example (DSL)
UMIF Operator CRD (YAML)

Automated Sanctions Engine — decision (Python)
Global Audit API consumer (TypeScript)
GIEN event publisher (Node.js)
PCR/PCO repair driver (Python)
codeExamples.snippet:
def repair(policy, invariants):
    issues = check(policy, invariants)
    if not issues: return policy
    suggestions = smt_synthesize(policy, issues)
    refactored  = llm_safe_refactor(policy, suggestions)
    proof = prove_or_harness(refactored, invariants)
    return sign_repair_envelope(policy, refactored, proof)

executiveSummary.purpose: Deliver comprehensive, expert-level guidance on designing and implementing an integrated Enterprise AI Governance, Risk, and Compliance stack for G-SIFI / Fortune 500 financial institutions (2026-2030), spanning ISO/IEC 42001 AIMS Manual, audit-defensible Model Risk Policy + MRM platform, AGI containment stack (SRASE + Sentinel AGI Containment Lab + red-team), and global civilizational AI governance (treaty 2026-2035, Global Audit API, Cert Scoring, GIEN, Auto Sanctions, Constitution, Codex, Transparency Portal, Cultural Resonance Archive, CSE-X, Invariance + Meta-Invariance + Epistemic + Ontological + Existential + Value alignment, UMIF, Self-Proving Systems + Policy DSL with Coq/TLA+/Z3/OPA + PCR/PCO repair, and the Minimal Governance Kernel with ≥ 10 000-attack adversarial break harness).
executiveSummary.approach: 14-module reference with machine-parsable directive, signed via Sigstore + ML-DSA-44/65 hybrid, enforced by OPA Gatekeeper + Cilium + MGK runtime, observed by Sentinel + eBPF + Cognitive Resonance, verified by Coq + TLA+ + Z3 (UMIF), audited by 3LoD + SRASE + Global Audit API, and operationalised through MVAGS at Day-90 extending to a 5-year roadmap with auto-assembled evidence pack ≤ 45 min for any regulator/auditor.
executiveSummary.deliverables: 14 modules · 70 sections · 12 schemas · 16 code examples · 6 case studies · 24 supervisory KPIs · 12 risk-control rows · 12 regulators · 7 workshops · 6 data flows · 14 traceability rows · 3-phase 30/60/90 · 5-year roadmap · machine-parsable <directive> block · evidence-pack template · ISO 42001 Cl 4-10 manual · Annex A control catalog · Model Risk Policy · UMIF + Self-Proving Systems + Policy DSL + MGK + adversarial break harness.
outcomes: ISO 42001 Stage 2 audit passed with 0 major NCs
MGK in production for all Tier-1 with ≥ 95 % proof coverage and 0 harness failures
SEV-0 logical kill-switch p95 ≤ 60 s; physical (BMC) ≤ 5 min
Annex IV / SR 11-7 pack assembly ≤ 30 min; evidence pack ≤ 45 min
SRASE composite ≥ 0.9 sustained before any real regulator submission
Cognitive Resonance Δ_drift ≤ 4 % + latent drift ≤ 3 % + cosine ≥ 0.92
Cert score Gold by 2027 and Platinum by 2029
Treaty obligations 100 % attested monthly; Global Audit API live; GIEN integrated
MGK adversarial break harness ≥ 10 000 attacks / release with 0 failures
